summary: Better than nuclear baseline due to topic's natural grounding in economic data. Material conditions present with concrete examples (deficit numbers, zoning percentages, Tokyo/Houston comparisons) but not systematically labeled or organized as the foundation of each argument. Contradictions are identified (supply vs regulation, feedback loop) but framed conversationally rather than as structural tensions. Dependencies are traced in Round 2-3 (investor causality, filtering chain) but without explicit chain notation or broken-link identification. Resolutions tend toward 'we need supply reform' rather than specifying which material condition changes and what measurable outcome follows. The Skeptic's feedback loop in Round 2 is the closest to genuine dialectical structure.
